get.summary: Get a list of audit logs
get.description: Get the list of audit logs for a specific environment, with optional filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The part of the keyword/comment of an audit log

parameters.name: CreatorId
parameters.description: The creator id

parameters.name: RefId
parameters.description: The referenced resource id, such as feature flag id or segment id

parameters.name: RefType
parameters.description: The referenced resource type. Can be one of the following: "FeatureFlag" or "Segment"

parameters.name: From
parameters.description: The start time of the audit log creation time range, in unix milliseconds

parameters.name: To
parameters.description: The end time of the audit log creation time range, in unix milliseconds

parameters.name: CrossEnvironment
parameters.description:
Whether to query logs across environments. By default, we only query logs in the specified environment (the current environment).
Only set this to true if you want to query logs for a shared-segment.

parameters.name: PageIndex
parameters.description: The index of the page to be returned. Default is 0.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: The size of the page to be returned. Default is 10.

parameters.name: Organization
parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.name: Workspace
parameters.description: The workspace ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the workspace context.

parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.description: The workspace ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the workspace context.

post.description: Create a new environment with the given name, key and description.

parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.name: Workspace

get.summary: Get flag list of an environment
get.description: Get the list of flags of a particular environment.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name/key or part of the name/key of a feature flag

parameters.name: Tags
parameters.description: The list of tags, you must use the complete name of tags

parameters.name: IsEnabled
parameters.description:
Return only enabled feature flags if true, and return only disabled feature flags if false.
If you don't provide any value, both enabled and disabled feature flags would be returned.

parameters.name: IsArchived
parameters.description: Return only archived feature flags if true, the default value is false

parameters.name: SortBy
parameters.description: The field to sort by, the default is createdAt

parameters.name: PageIndex
parameters.description: The index of the page to be returned. Default is 0.

parameters.name: PageSize
parameters.description: The size of the page to be returned. Default is 10.

parameters.name: Organization
parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.name: Workspace
parameters.description: The workspace ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the workspace context.

post.description: Create a feature flag with the given name, key, and description.

parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.name: Workspace
parameters.description: The workspace ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the workspace context.

parameters.description: The organization ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the organization context.

parameters.name: Workspace
parameters.description: The workspace ID associated with the request. Some APIs may require this header to identify the workspace context.

parameters.name: Workspace
